title: Unix timestamp 0 cannot be encoded as base_time
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/senml/senml.c
Lines: 75-78

CATEGORY: PROTOCOL

The senml_pack_init() function uses base_time > 0 to determine if base_time should be set:
  if (base_time > 0) {
      pack->base_time = base_time;
      pack->has_base_time = true;
  }

Unix timestamp 0 (January 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC) is a valid timestamp, but this code treats it as 'not set'.

RFC 8428 Section 4.2 states base_time is an optional field that can be any numeric value. There is no prohibition against timestamp 0.

IMPACT:
- Cannot encode events that occurred at Unix epoch
- Historical data from embedded systems that initialized clocks to 0 cannot be properly timestamped
- Unlikely in practice but violates RFC

FIX: Change API to accept a bool parameter explicitly indicating whether base_time should be included, or use UINT64_MAX as sentinel, or accept a pointer to optional base_time.
